sanskrit:
साङ्ख्ययोगौ पृथग् बाला: प्रवदन्ति न पण्डिता:।
एकमप्यास्थितः सम्यङ् उभयोर्विन्दते फलम्‌॥
transliteration: sāṅkhya-yogau pṛthag bālāḥ pravadanti na paṇḍitāḥ | ekam āpy āsthitaḥ samyaṅ ubhayor vindate phalam ||
literal_meaning: The childish say Sāṅkhya and Yoga are separate; the wise do not. Established well in even one, a person gains the fruit of both.
contextual_meaning: All authentic growth-paths converge; splitting them is a cognitive defense that keeps the ego feeling special but stalled.
psychological_state: Cognitive splitting resolved by experiential proof that sustained practice on any single coherent path delivers the same inner freedom.
trigger_scenario: Debating whether therapy, meditation, or service is the 'real' way to heal while avoiding actually doing any of them consistently.
anchor_text: Pick one path and walk it fully; every true path leads to the same summit.
